verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation, with its kinase and SH2/SH3 domains facing the cytoplasm. No contextual bucket applies: (a) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet exposure on tumor cells, dying cells, or activated cells; (b) tissue_restricted_surface — not displayed ecto-facing in germline/developmental lineages; (c) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM cargo; (d) dual_localization — endosomal/perinuclear pools exist but all cytoplasmic-facing, no outer-leaflet pool; (e) stable_surface_attachment — not tethered to any TM partner on the ecto face. Clinical SRC inhibitors (dasatinib, bosutinib) are intracellular ATP-competitive small molecules, consistent with cytoplasmic-face localization.
